reviews.review_msg: Nice prof! His excellent notes make everything clear. Assignments are not hard but useful. Easy midterm and final :)
Finally a math prof that can actually teach. His notes were fantastic. Homework was actually useful homework.
Amazing prof, really knows his stuff

If you're not a math major, and it's been awhile since you've seen math maybe cuz of coop or something, lectures become really hard to understand, like very difficult. Sat there scratching my head. But if you go to OH, get help, he'll explain things to you well and you'll get a good understanding for exams, the computing reports and whatnot.

It was a difficult course and you need to put in a fair amount of effort, but he tried his best to make it related to real-life/practical problems and the purpose of the course clear. The computing reports were like puzzles but interesting, you can get help during OH and tutorials. He needs to be aware of cheating during quizzes/exams though.

I think he's a good prof and try to make the class interesting by relating the concepts to real life use cases. His notes have mistakes sometimes but it's math so I think it's normal.
MACM 316 is hard to understand, if you're not into math like me. I'm on the edge of failing this course if not curved, but tbh I still think JF is good lmao

John is a fairly good prof, lectures aren't too bad, pretty engaging. quizzes are built off of textbook hw (optional). assignments marked by TAs,beware of hard marking. Both exams are OK, just understand quizzes.

I think John is a good professor, the subject was simply not that interesting. Quiz and exam questions usually-ish reflected what was in the homework, but he made the multiple choice tricky. The grading criteria for the computing assignments was unclear and marks would be docked for missing things they didn't even include in the description.

John is enthusiastic, but the course itself is difficult. Things to keep in mind: 1) Homework and Quiz: HW is not mandatory, but you MUST do it to perform well on the weekly quizzes - these are annoying, but helpful later on when revising. 2) Attendance is required (5%). 3) Fair exams - but PLEASE READ THE QUESTION CAREFULLY BEFORE ANSWERING

John was very enthusiastic, but he would also give trick questions on all multiple choice. He would state that they were “easy questions” that we have learned all semester, but purposely not state the conditions needed for it to be true, even though we spent all semester assuming the conditions were there. HW not needed. Quizzes WAY too long.

Never enough time to finish quizzes. Strict grading on computing assignments. Makes you buy an iClicker subscription for participation marks. Also discourages questions mid-lecture and is condescending if you don't know something. Had to drop after midterm, otherwise he would have cooked my GPA

I've sat in my friends' MACM316 lectures and classes before that isn't John and to be honest, after reflecting and being in pain in this class because it's hard, John is legitimately your best option in terms of fairness. This class is hard and I don't disagree with the other reviews and viewpoints, all valid.

Straight forward points:
1. Probably your best option for MACM316
2. HW not mandatory but kinda is if you want to do well on quizzes / exams
3. Fair tests but also time-consuming (e.g., decimals, hard derivatives, etc) ALTHOUGH marking is questionable and read question very well before answring
4. Good lectures
5. No BS prof but strict on reasoning

Not the best, not the worst. Lectures are very good, but he often gives tricky questions with multiple correct answers when only one is acceptable and sometimes tested us on material not covered in the lectures/homework. Somewhat nice, but also blamed us multiple times for not getting "easy" questions correct and didn't take accountability.

He is really passionate about teaching and a great lecturer. This course is just pretty hard and time consuming. Weekly quizzes keep you on top of the content but are a pretty hard for 10 minutes. Avg of class was a C+. All things considered he's a good prof for macm316!
Had a really great semester with John. Sometimes he pulls tricks out of nowhere which is annoying but none the less he is a good lecturer and really loves teaching

Other people are saying he "curved us up a letter grade" I would like to confirm this wasn't the case for most people as he curved 1-2% only so it didnt change most people's grades. He is good at teaching and smart, take the course with him if you care about macm but don't take the course if you want a good grade.
John is the best!!

The homework is so useless and too difficult. None of the homework really gets tested anyways. Just focus on the lecture notes and clickers. I did so bad on the final exam because I didn't know there was an iClicker bank with lots of questions word by word on the final exam.

Marks very strictly on computing assignments and quizzes are too long for 10 mins and very tricky. Midterm mc was tricky written was fine. Final mc was extremely fair and written was a lot for a no calculator exam. Overall he isn't the best but its clear he likes what he is teaching. He also curved us up a grade which was nice given how tough it is

Nice guy after all, he is very passionate about teaching this class. Would recommend to take if you want to actually learn something. Put in minimal effort and still passed. Used chatGPT for all my computing assignments and averaged out to 75%.

To be honest, he isn't as bad as he seems. It was a rough semester with tricky multiple choice questions, long weekly quizzes that are not reasonable for a 10 minute time period. Midterm was long but was alright. Final multiple choice wasn't so tricky but written was unreasonably hard (not because of the content) but because of the numbers provided

Curved letter grade doesn't excuse all the BS we faced. Useless homework that didn't prepare you for the weekly quizzes with trick questions. Computing assignments marked out of 10 where you lose marks for not explaining things PERFECTLY. Final exam's paper size was taller to cram in more questions. Not worth damaging your mental health over.
Had a fun and engaging semester with John. His quizzes sometimes can be a lot at times. Computing assignments are chill as long as you start early. Class avg was a C+ and he also gave bonus marks.
To be fair, John isn't the worst at SFU. He does make mistakes during lectures but it is understandable he is getting old but very passionate about teaching.
I was supposed to receive an A+ but only got an A. John did not curve me up, why is everyone lying.

I know his rating is low, but he's genuinely not that bad. He clearly loves the topic, clearly knows what he's talking about, and the lectures are actually useful. 

Attend every lecture and you'll be good.
Wasn't even so bad at all. John made the class hard and tricky but he compensated us with a 1 letter grade curve. Take with John if you want to succeed.
Happy to say that I have finally passed MACM 316 with John on my retake! Bless you John!
And John he the GOAT! I be ducking all the pigs. I turn up at my shows
John curved even when he said he would not. This is a christmas miracle!

John isn't a bad professor after all! He understands that it is a hard class. His final was fine (Glad most of his MC were just clicker stuff and concepts). Written could have been more reasonable given no calculators were allowed.
I am the one who got 95 on the final exam
Great professor! Not the worst and not the very best but he is nice and gave everyone a bonus letter grade

Great Lecturer, sub-par examiner. His lectures are very in-depth, and you learn a lot. His weekly quizzes and exams weren't overly fair, with no real way to practice and MCQ was half of your grade. If you can't do basic calculus and fractions without a calculator, he's definitely not the prof for you.
We were way too harsh towards John earlier in the semester. He really does care about students and made our grade higher! Good professor!!
John is not so bad after all, he made the final exam out of 95 instead of 100 and he also boosted everyone's grade. I thought I was getting a C- but got a C.

Not the worst prof I've ever had, but definitely not good either. His lectures are above average, you can tell he cares about the material. Mandatory iClicker for lectures aren't a problem, weekly quizzes get pretty difficult but represent test material. Final was 17 longer than usual pages (so like 23), midterm was fair. Overall very mid prof.

Worst part is his assessments and grading. Hw given is hardly related to his weekly quizzes. There is no clear rubric for the assignments worth 25%, losing marks for the smallest details. Blames students for quiz/midterms for terrible averages. His actual lectures, however, are more engaging/interesting than average, relative to SFU's CS profs.
The most fun course I've ever taken!

Really passionate and enthusiastic prof. Go to the first lecture and see if you like the way he thinks/teaches.

The material is pretty straightforward and strongly related to the weekly quizzes and the midterm/final.
If you can get 10/10 on the quizzes, you'll be golden for both MT/FINAL.

The final exam was not difficult concept wise, but for a non calculator exam, he sure put alot of floating point numbers and fractions. Furthermore, he doesn't provide any sample exams and the textbook questions are useless to practice for his exam format. Would not recommend!

Final exam was brutal, written section was much more advanced compared to the lecture notes and quizzes. Multiple choice supposedly came from a “clicker bank” which was never shared by John. Looks like we are being punished for something outside of our control!

The marking on the exams are unreasonable, just look at previous comments. The final exam written was absolutely brutal today, h(x) = 1 + (x-1)^2 / (1+6x^4) derivative + a decimal input? PLU matrix + gauss-siedel matrix iteration? Yeah, okay.

Final exam, no calculator, had multiple long answer questions with fraction/decimal calculations taking up time while you still had to do 25 MC questions, each worth 2 (no part marks), some also requiring full-on formula setups and fraction calculations. Few people could have realistically completed this in time and correctly, he wants you to fail.

His lectures are engaging and go into depth about the topic. He does not upload lecture notes and quiz's are based on the concepts not the homework questions. If you work to understand the material in the slides none of the asessments feels unfair; however the quiz time limits are much too short .Other than that he is great.
John please let me pass macm 316

Let me just put a quiz that's not possible to solve in 10 minutes. In addition, let me make a MT matching question that had 0 indication to explain your answer and deduct -1 point for all students. Oh, and also let me make a quiz that says 'circle one answer per question' but needed 3 correct answers to get full marks which also wasn't indicated.
He's just a really poor teacher. This guy cannot educate.
You know something is about to turn sour when quiz marks get released too fast or too late. It is always the class average is low.

John: "Guys it's your fault for failing the quiz you should have done the homework."

The homework: "What's similar between Gaussian elimination and the ancient Chinese technique developed by Liu Hui in his book Jiu Zhang Suan Shu?"

The quizzes are way too hard. Nothing is related to the homework he gives, he proceeds to gaslight us that it comes from the homework. How many opps you really got? I mean, it's too many options
I'm finna pass on this body, I'm John Stockton. Take with another prof.

Dont bother reading all the reviews down this message, its just basically the same opinion which ill conclude in this message.
This prof expects student to solve a quiz that is no way possible to be solved in 10 mins. The Format of exams is super unfair. The assignment takes time and at the end its graded unfairly. People literally failing with him
Not recommended to take this course with this professor. Better off with other professors!! Hope that helps!

Absolutely ridiculous class, his lowest quiz average was the most recent one with 39% average. Never taken Math classes with such a low quiz failing average. We all know who has to be blamed for such poor results. Nothing much we can do, just pray hard for the final exam. I worked hard, Prof Stockie, I did HW, result still bad.

He's a nice guy, but his style of teaching is not right. Lecture attendance is mandatory because he does live clicker questions, and he also doesn't post the filled in lecture slides and writes a load of unreadable equations on them and goes through them quickly. Hw/quizzes are not fair or fairly graded. Homework is often irrelevant to exams.

Avoid this prof at all costs. The most stressful course with unfair marking, misleading instructions for Quiz/midterm questions which he keeps revising the answers for. He does not share his annotated notes, blames students for not understanding. It's almost like he wants you to suffer and fail the course. The most demotivating class I've taken.

He criticizes people for not doing the homework to prepare for the quizzes, yet he throws in random quiz questions that weren't even covered on the homework. He wastes your time by giving you useless textbook questions to do, where half of them can't even be quizzed because they would require you to run Matlab code on a computer. Super unfair.

Had to drop this course, absolutley awful professor, gave an impossible quiz which absolutley NOBODY finished. This guy just wants to see his students suffer and doesn't care about them at all, dropped this course I'm outta here and going to work at Mcdonald's rather than listen to his yap and deal with his BS.

His quizzes are always stressful, rushing to finish in 10 mins. I knew how to do them but did not have enough time to complete, so sad. Hoping to get more marks from the quizzes but my hope is down to the drain. Thanks Stockie for giving quiz questions that require more than 10 mins to finish. I worked so hard and didn't get what I hope for. SAD!!

Yet again today he gives a quiz with heavy amounts of questions where it is newrly
Impossible to complete properly within a 10 minute time frame. F#%^* you John.

Forces attendance with iClickers. Doesn't post notes, so if you're sick you either miss content or risk spreading illness (or worse come healthy and get sick). Weekly quizzes make people show up sick (lectures felt like a clinic). His “generous” policy lets you miss 1 of 11 quizzes, any more is your problem. After midterm, less than half attended.
Doesnt't know how to properly grade assignments, too much marks taken off for nothing

John's computing assignments are graded really unfairly. Even when you follow all the instructions and include everything required, the TAs or John still say parts are missing. It's frustrating because the expectations are clear, yet the marking doesn't match. The feedback feels inconsistent and honestly makes the course stressful.

This professor marks super harshly, even if you mostly get the question right. One small mistake and you lose almost everything. It's really discouraging, especially when the professor makes multiple mistakes during lectures. Like, why are we graded so strictly when the teaching isn't even consistent? It just doesn't feel fair.

The TAs grade computing assignments terribly. Even when you clearly include everything required, they still claim it's missing. It's frustrating and unfair because the expectations are written clearly, but the marking never matches. The feedback feels inconsistent and makes it hard to know what they actually want.

This professor grades extremely harshly. Even if you use the right method, one small algebra mistake can make you lose almost all marks. The marking feels unfair and discouraging. They also make several mistakes during lectures, which adds to the confusion. Overall, it's a frustrating and demotivating experience.

The quiz are unreasonably difficult and he wastes a lot of time in class teaching about stuff that doesn't matter or is not covered in the course. There is no material to prepare for exams and his homework questions to prepare for the quiz are useless, as most of the time they are too difficult to solve by hand and need code.

This class is frustrating. Professor gives tricky and time consuming question in a 10 minutes quiz. In such a time constraints, careless mistakes is unavoidable when students have to rush to finish. TA took marks off instantly when the answer is wrong. No part marks for doing everything right except wrong answers coz of careless mistakes.

Other than putting trick questions where students have to argue for points, marking scheme is terrible especially at 2 marks per multiple choice question, where almost every single one is tricky or ambiguous. He puts a 3 mark coding question and gives 0 part marks, this does not make sense at all. Making the question worth 1 mark would be more fair
Absolutely terrible, don't know why he is still teaching. I would not take this class in general if it was not required for my computer science degree.

Take it with Steven Ruuth, last semester his class midterm average was over 70% and eventhough final's average was a little over 40% but he curved 2 letter grades. Stockie midterm average 50% and maybe final's average will be even lower, and he is not curving. Does not make sense! Stockie, your test questions aren't make sense either.

Tough quiz and midterm, and expect the same for final exam. There are >50 students failed midterm. Yet, professor Stockie said he is not going to curve. Not curving in 3rd level course is unacceptable especially when he put unreasonable or tricky questions on the test. Steven Ruuth who taught this class did curve. So unfair, we didn't get curve.

This professor had to reverse the solutions that he had for quiz and midterm after student challenged his solutions. If the professor made questions that he himself couldn't answer it correctly then those questions should be removed. His accuses of overlooked and blah...totally no accepted. Shame! My advice, do not take this course with him.

I just feel sad because I worked so hard I studied for the midterm countless hours and the midterm was unfair. Its not right to have trick multiple choice questions each worth 2 points out of a 30 point total exam. Each had multiple correct answers, I'm under time pressure I can't spent years on your mind games. I usually do well in midterms. Sad.

Multiple choice questions are structured to screw you over. The questions are very vague and he expects you to know thats the answer he wants even if there were also other correct answers. Note the question did not say "Pick the best answer" or anything like that. He blames us for not doing well.Wonder if the instructor made the questions "well" 🖕
Puts a bunch of trick questions in the midterm's multiple choice and then blames us for doing badly on them.

Avoid this professor at all cost for this course. Confusions and errors during the lecture and yet, challenges us with difficult quizzes and midterm. Come on the quiz is only worth 1.5% each and not necessarily to make it hard and long which often needs more than 10 mins to complete it correctly.

Half of the things in the lectures are useless and are never tested, makes errors often, confusing students. When the class did bad on a quiz, he blames the class for not understanding the material. But guess who is the one that failed to teach the material?
If I got a dollar for each time John Stockie gives a question that is not related to the homework I would be rich.

he'll explain coding or computers, but ends up telling us the wrong information, and when someone questions him on it, he says "i don't actually know, i'm not a computer scientist." he often makes huge mistakes, and ends up re-explaining and saying "ignore everything i just said" which is such a waste of time. answers questions very condescendingly

Homework problems are designed to waste your time as majority of it never shows on the quiz. Problems assigned are sometimes not even relevant to what is being tested in the course. Quizzes are designed to lower your mark in the class since he doesn't curve or scale
Being difficult for no reason
Horrible, that's all I can say.

John is a very knowledgeable professor and it shows in his lectures. My only compliant is that he isn't very approachable. Whenever questions were asked in class, he would cut off the person asking to provide a condescending half-answer. I also found some of the quiz marking to be questionable, however this was not the case for the midterm or final
Amazing professor. The lectures were clear, fair exams. I learned a lot in his macm 316 class.
An amazing prof. I would highly recommend taking MACM 316 with John.

MACM316 is known as a hard class, however with John "ACADEMIC COMBACK" Stockie, this class was much more bearable than intended. Lectures are clear and concise with mandatory IClickers throughout the weeks, very light hearted and hilarious guy. John always has methods to get "free marks". 5/5 Prof.

John is probably the best math teacher I've ever had. His class is funny and easily to understand. He prepared carefully before each class and patiently answered questions raised by every student.I can say MACM316 is one of my favourite classes I have ever taken because my professor is John Stockie.
Lectures are copied from the textbook. Almost weekly quizzes and computing assignments. Quizzes are based on homework questions. Midterm was hard. Class average: 44%. Final was ok.

His midterm was designed to basically be impossible to finish in the 50 minutes given but his final was very fair (took questions straight from hw/quizzes). His lectures focus a lot on theory/proofs but he tests largely on application making his lectures at times pointless to even attend. Overall nice guy but expect to work hard.

his lectures are very unclear and monotone, midterm and assignment are super difficult, and final is "Hall Of Fame" (if you play NBA 2K, you know what I mean). The worst part is he didn't do any curve or scale, which makes over 50% failing the class. AVOID AT ALL COSTS!!!

Expected a better grade, a bit surprised. But he made the course easier on us, work wise, and we learned the important stuff. Isn't too strict about the cheat sheet, which is nice cause it resulted in less memorizing. Not the best comunicator, Ralf W. is better, but he's still good enough to learn well from.

If you have to take MACM 316, Steven Ruuth is THE MAN! He takes what would have otherwise been a dry and hellish GPA killer into an interesting and enjoyable class. He is one of - no, he IS the best prof I have had at this university. I can't say enough good things about this prof!
naw i cannot wtih this course
Jane is a very good lecturer, making Numerical Analysis much easier to handle.
no. Nope. Run.

Just don't. Bad lectures slides. Tests are asking completely different things from what she mentioned in the lectures. She asked to memorize the MATLAB syntax the whole term, and then never asked a question about it on the final. The course is doable tho, but there are much better profs to take it  with.

She often loses her train of thought and just follows her notes. She doesn't post her filled out lecture slides and she doesn't allow people to take pictures without her permission. Also, the optional homework doesn't relate to the quizzes/exams. If you go to office hours she is really nice and actually makes sense

Exams and quizzes are unrelated to the textbook homework. Focus on her lecture slides and be prepared to memorize all the formulas and small details. If something doesn't make sense on the slides, there's a high chance she's made a mistake. Don't expect much from her; she's not capable of explaining well. Mahdi, her TA, is much better at teaching.

This prof has terrible lecture notes and cannot teach effectively enough for an upper division Math course such as MACM 316. It's passable so long as you prepare for the final and do fine on the assignments. I would see the TA's frequently here. She isn't good enough of a teacher to help you excel so you have to do the work yourself and see TA's.

Jane is not a competent teacher at all. She is always late, doesn't allow students to take pictures of the slides, and just generally doesn't care for her students. Quizzes were difficult and unreasonable, with way too much content to memorize in this class, since no formula sheets were allowed. Clearly she's only here for research. Please avoid!
Does not test materials in lecture and homework, very messy writing, and does not upload lecture notes.

Completely uninvested in her student's. Doesn't post the automatically recorded lectures. Doesn't post filled in notes. Her handwriting is very bad with lots of mistakes. She yells at you if you take pictures, but if you ask sometimes it's fine? No practice midterm or final. No cheat sheets, and the midterm was mostly proofs with no warning. Avoid.

Frequently gets lost in her own notes and makes countless mistakes during lectures, greatly disrupting learning. Yells at students for taking picture of the projector during class. Refuses to post filled in lecture notes but lectures too fast for students to copy down the notes in class. Be prepared to memorize formulas. Power trip issues.

She had the TAs stood over our shoulders, stared at us for almost the entire exam to send a message. None of us were even thinking of cheating. The exam was already hard, and we didn't need that kind of pressure.. Just don't take any course with her..

I wish there is "Unrespectable" in the categories above. She is so far the least respected professors I know by all of her students, and for valid reasons. Unsupportive is one thing, but she is actively sabotaging. Ugly, illegible notes. Gate keeping. Uneven marking. Random 2 minutes long pause. Rude. 100% of her effort is spent on her outfit.
Worst of all time indeed.

She might be a stellar researcher but she absolutely sux at teaching. SFU please do better and have capable personnel teach such an important course. I was made to lose interest and stop caring about this course very early on. TBH she might have been the worst I've had of all time.

Qzs assig n final were fair but the midterm was brutal. Professor Jane, as a relatively new professor, you've received criticism, and I agree with some of it. I suggest revising your teaching strategies(note taking, lecture recds) I'm all in for tough exams, but please teach the material you expect us to know. We're paying a lot to be educated.

Being taught by Jane feels like reading a textbook. She has no passion and hates teaching.She also litterly dosent have a tablet and like when she records stuff she writes with her mouse. Like SFU GET HER AN IPAD. She gate keeps resources too

The only thing good thing about Jane is her outfits, she inspires me in dress to impress with my baddies.

Makes mistakes while writing in lectures but easily solves doubts right after the class. Does not record lectures. Expected us to memorize all topics with tedious formulas. Provided homework focuses on MATLAB while questions in quiz or exams expect us to use that algorithm by hand. Do yourself a favor, don't take this course with her.
Yep, the exam is 100% rote memorization. To the nitty gritty details. And if you get the nitty gritty details wrong, whole mark for the entire question is gone.

Avoid. Lectures are somehow fast yet filled with stammers and errors. Notes only done on projector. Only provided notes are useless skeletons meant to be filled out in class. Bad handwriting and removes page from projector as soon as she's done writing. Berates students for taking pictures of projector slides.

Unprofessional lecture notes and teaching (you don't know how to use beamer?) + she speaks perfect English yet she has a talent to make her words more than hard to understand + REDICULOUS TEST QUESTIONS COMPARE TO THE TOPICS AND WHAT SHE ACTUALLY DELIVERS IN CLASS.

The class is not for you to shine prof, it's a place for students to learn.
RUNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNN

Reviewing for the final really highlights how many mistakes she made scribbling out her notes on a projector. You have to copy notes so fast, you can't actually think about what you're writing, so you hope it makes sense later, and half the time, it doesn't, and of course there's no reference material or recording of her making these mistakes.

- superiority complex
- power trip issues
- stubborn
- atrocious notes
- not great at explaining
- unfair tests
- unreasonable marking deductions
- SFU you're better than this, please fire her
Worse than Randall Pyke, arguably worst in SFU math department
She doesn't give annotated notes and expects everyone to be in class. There is no exam cheat sheet, and the student wants to remember everything, even the code.
The five star reviews for this professor are written by herself. Don't be fooled. Listen to the masses. Other professors at SFU don't receive backlash like this.

Teaching isn't the worst among Math department, obviously still a million miles away from Jamie Mulholland, but you can tell that she tries. However she does need therapy. I have nothing against her, yet I still think her yelling at the class and threatening legal actions is ridiculous. Oh and the separate "4-function-calculator".

Feel bad for her? Did she feel bad when she acted like a utter tyrant in the class on and on about copy right and academic violation just for couple of pictures? Did she feel bad when she ignored us, ignored our homeworks, and treated us like we are invisible? Did she feel bad when she called us incompetent and lazy? Or for the inappropriate shirt?

A true disasterclass on teaching a course. Students constantly correcting her mistakes in the middle of class. The actual lecture notes are skeleton notes and can only be filled in during class as she does not post her annotated slides or lecture recordings online. Tests happen to only focus on the lecture slides. Weird power trip too, seek help.

This professor is one of the worst professor at SFU. Aside from her unfair grading, no matter how much work you put in, she will not reward you with the grades you deserve. The topics in this class are not bad but she has gone out of her way to drop the class average. No teaching skills and is extremely rude and unprofessional. Avoid at all cost.
Judging from the amounts of ratings Jane gives herself on this site and the amount of name callings she gives her students, it's fair to think some weird there is some narcissism tendencies  going on

She is not the worst professor performance wise, but she has a weird obsession with power tripping, gate kipping and yelling. Wavering and volatile emotion. Your gradings is totally dependent on her mood and who you are. Go to show what university budget cut can do to us

Stop writing yourself poems Jane, they are cringey. Maybe if you had focused a little bit more on caring, they wouldn't have bashed you so hard. Learn from your mistakes and move on, treat the next cohort with more care and less dictatorship.

Mistakes in notes and computing assignments, illegible writing, scribbles over mistakes, students cannot take pictures of any notes, does not post annotated slides, no formula sheets, no midterm solutions, clueless during lecture and just copies off her notes. Her voice also makes my ears bleed.

Is she out to fail you? No. Is she good at teaching MACM 316? No. Dr. MacDonald seems nice but is hindered by her lack of good teaching notes. Assignments contained typos which were a pain to rewrite with new results in the report. She might be good with other courses but MACM 316 is notoriously hard and these things don't help her.

Poor time management skills, an inability to explain concepts well, and making tons of mistakes during lectures, which really confuses students. Simply being "nice" doesn't make someone a good professor, especially in a subject like this.

Dr. MacDonald is highly regarded for her contributions to the research field. Her lectures on Numerical Analysis provide a comprehensive exploration of the subject and introduce various numerical methods. If you are afraid of putting in the work... do not bother!

Here is a more realistic review:no pics of slides due to copyright. Says you can ask but she sometimes says no.Lots of ways to get around this.Very kind and easy to talk to in OH;gives back points for quizzes/exams fairly easily if disputed.Midterm/quizzes completely based off understanding of concepts;not hard quizzes either.She is really not bad.

I wouldn't mind if she were strict but organized and knew how to teach properly. The problem is that she is extremely disorganized and doesn't know how to teach at all, yet she is very strict with students and expects a lot from us. Literally the worst kind of professor. I wish I had taken this course with Brenda. I could have learned so much more.

Class is a mess, does not know what is she talking about, writes fast, no taking pictures, does not explain calculations, and no filled notes or any lectures, this class is self studying but is really hard to teach yourself since there is not that much online sources for the topics we're studying. Do your self a favor and take it with someone else.

To say that "you are just lazy" and "you just don't put in work" to explain away Jane's authoritarian approach toward students is simply minimizing for a lot of us out here trying to cope with 4 courses per semester while paying international tuition fee. Her writings and lecturing are bad, speaking is unclear with a lot of stammering, that's fact
Why the hat?

Cares too much about looking pretty and being a petty tyrant and too little about students' well being. Verbal expression in class borderline harassment. Dressed up funny at best and inappropriate at worst.

Hi, SFU Department of Mathematics, 
Jane has the potential to be an excellent researcher. Please don't waste her talent and time by letting her teach macm 316.

Always on a power trip - tells students not to take pictures of her slides, and another day saying "you can ask to take photos - yes, go ahead, if you ask I'll let you." She dressed as princess peach for no reason one time, idk why. She explains concepts poorly, distracting from core concepts, and assigns homework on material not taught in class.
Why does she keep treating classroom like a runway?

She really enjoys power tripping over you. Her markings is absolutely racially targeted. Just need some of my friends to speak up and planning to take her to court. She also verbally harassed her students alot in class, no wonder she doesn't allow cameras.

if you guys think that she doesn't monitor this page, you are wrong. She is spending time not to prepare for lecture slide, but to monitor her rmp and compile a list of "suspected students" that she would dock marks from. Stay safe out there you guys, your money is now being used against you.

Her lectures are indeed amazing. She would be talking, then abruptly stop talking for 5 minutes to look at the filled note to figure out what was going on. Also, copyright my ass. She stole her Matlab code from a guy who wrote it in 2015. Even forgot to delete that line. Even her lecture note content are stolen.
RUN

Sometimes when i sit in class I wonder if jane rents out her brain as a speed bump on the weekends. She lacks understanding of what she is teaching so much so, she pauses for 3-4 minutes just to collect herself. someone should really stop her from stealing perfectly good oxygen from all the other forms of intelligent life on this planet

Jane asks that all her students bring a 'simple calculator' for exams. which is reasonable until she says no to my mickey mouse singing calculator. such discrimination should not be allowed in a classroom. jane should be fired not only for her inability to teach, but also for the fact she is duller than a box of crayons

Jane acts like her lectures are a fashion show. so far, the OOTD has consisted of 
- baseball player
- sadness from inside out
- zebra
- blends in with classroom

I honestly dont know where she's getting these outfits from

Jane has no respect for her students or anyone for that matter. when asked if she can zoom in on her projector her answer was 'no sorry I can't' sometimes she makes me wonder how a professor with at least one PHD can have room temperature IQ
sometimes when jane teaches, I wonder if she is hard of thinking.

If I knew my MACM 316 professor would be Jane Macdonalds, I literally would have quit school to work at mcdonalds instead. Please save your selves and your money and DO NOT take this course under any circumstance.

I know SFU is undergoing budget cuts but I didn't know how bad it was until I met jane. I'm sure jane is a very smart individual outside the classroom. however, as an individual, Jane is quite talented at making a classroom of students mock her behind her back without her knowing.

Jane has the exceptional ability to make a simple concept seem like rocket science. she could probably confuse a monkey on how to open a banana. Jane can even confuse herself on the concept she is suppose to teach. SFU needs to step up their game and hire qualified profs

She asks her students to put up their hands to ask permission to take pictures of her lectures. its a waste of time, disturbs the flow of class. and everyone hates her for it. if you want to see someone dig their own grave. take this class.

Jane has no lecture recordings or filled out notes. she prays for her students to fail her class. and hope they fail it miserably. She deletes her zoom lecture recordings a week after they are posted. if that is not praying on our downfall. I dont know what is. save yourselves. do not take this class with her.

Tough lectures, tough class, someone needs to get jane her meds for whatever mental illness she has. girliepop is out here with so much confidence that her lectures are about to be 'stolen'.... by who? other professors? bestie no one wants your slides. they're more confusing than a kindergartener's coloring book. get it together Jane.

Only the 1 star comments are true. Jane dresses as a different movie character every day, for example, she was dressed as princess peach last week for no reason. I wonder what kind of person has the confidence to show up to work. in front of 200 students. in a bright. pink. costume. dont take if you value your sanity.

Jane is an absolute maniac in the course. She berates her students for taking pictures of her lecture slides, commenting on how they are 'copyrighted'. keep in mind, no one would ever use these lecture slides, even if all traces of mathematics was erased from the world and this was the only thing left of it. avoid at all cost.
She's very good at jumping at the most negative conclusion of students. Take her class if you enjoy being yelled at.

She thought I was taking pictures of her notes, but I was actually taking a selfie. 
Anyway this is still a great class if you are interested in fashion or dressing.

Jane is a temperamental lecturer with wavering emotions. She has horrible handwriting, does not allow taking picture, has vague assignment questions. And don't get me started on her "one page report" when the assignment was 6 pages long. She was extremely rude during office hour, dismissive and sometime straight up refused to talk. $4000 wasted!

When you're horrible at teaching, please, at least do your best to make learning material accessible. Very childish, disorganized, and does not care about teaching quality, and instead, enforces extremely strict policies. I blame the university for allowing such a person to teach a course that includes important concepts for STEM major students.

Unfortunately, all the reviews posted here are accurate. She is extremely unprofessional and yells at students who are just trying to keep up with her horrible lectures and handwriting. I wish she understood that we just want to learn and provided the lecture material that we paid for. The reasons she won't do this remain unclear.

Avoid if you can. You need to go to class to fill in the notes but she goes too fast to follow along and copy down what you need. She gets mad if you take pictures on her slides and has a lot of "asides" in her work, yet if asked to go back to a slide so we can finish writing she refuses and says we dont have time.

Her attitude towards students will only create resentment. She makes note taking incredibly difficult, and then yells at us when students take pictures trying to keep up due to "copyright infringement". She's only making enemies for herself, and she doesn't support our learning. Some lecturers give their whole textbook away for free, in comparison.

It is unfortunate that she is insistent on making the class as difficult as possible. You must attend lectures. She refuses to use technology and her writing is horrendous. She adds unnecessary complexity to the material and makes mistakes in her notes OFTEN and does not inform the class of the mistakes. AVOID AT ALL COSTS.

she obviously did not have a clue what's going on during the lecture, but is also very arrogant. Often points out all the wrong things about the textbook and goes off a tangent. She jumps from a small section in one chapter to another and back. Hands down, a new level for bad teaching.
what a shame! Could have inspired people but no.

constantly wanders off her slides and lost her train of thought. Then made mistakes immediately. Always put the projectors on maximum brightness or split screens making it extremely small and hard to read, but never bothers to fix it. Arrogant, uncaring, unsupportive, borderline power tripping. She is indeed the worst at SFU for math.

I am a fairly hardworking student, and have never had any problem with math or stem subjects, but Jane has made this class so much harder than it has to be. She was a difficult professor, threatening to deduct all mark if you ever dare dispute gradings. She made "taking pictures" of her note in class an academic dishonesty. A horrible teacher.

I was considering on declaring a math minor, and after this course, I don't want it anymore. THE ABSOLUTE HORRIBLE, never been this excited to write on the course survey at the end of the semester, I have a lot to say. The fact that 20+ students gave a 1/5 in quality withtin the span of one month, should tell you something.

She is the worst professor i have ever taken. DO NOT, i mean NEVER take this course with her. Although she doesn't count attendance, you MUST go to class as she doesn't share her notes due to "copyright" issues. Her writing is as bad as a doctor, you are writing notes not prescription Jane. There are more to say but just DO NOT EVER TAKE HER.

Jane rambles in class often, and strays off her note, and usually makes a lot of mistake. Yet, often time she doesn't follow the textbook and criticizes it for it's lackings. Fair exam, but very compressed in time. She makes up a load of "academic violation" policies regarding her notes, which has never been seen before at SFU.

She is extremely unsupportive and gate keeping toward her material. Deletes note immediately after writing them so you can't copy them down. Taking pictures of her note in class is now an academic and copy right violation. In exams or quiz, if you forget to write "a)" you will lose mark for the whole question. Arbitrary marking tendencies.
Doesn't know what she's talking about half the time, hand writing is poor, questions on tests aren't related to homework questions, sucks in general

Arbitrary rules to gatekeep learning. No calculators. Only half-complete lecture slides posted, you must attend class to complete them. Barely follows textbook. Tests based on lecture slides. Uses projector, nothing digital. Doesn't know how to set up projector if it's small. Awful handwriting. Midterm was 1.5 hours of work in a 50 minute class.

This course with Jane is the most difficult course I've ever taken, and I'm in my last semester. Her quizzes are hard and irrelevant to the homework, marking is strict and she is super harsh about taking notes but talks fast, doesn't allow photos, and she wants students to fail. She took down lecture videos after 1 week with no notice.

She frequently stammers and garbles her words, often appearing unsure of what she's talking about. While I can tolerate her strict policies, SFU should ensure more competent lecturers, especially for key courses, even if enrollment is low. Being understaffed is one thing, but settling for inadequacy is another.

This is by far the most unprepared professor I've ever had. She shows no interest in the course and cannot teach it effectively. She consistently exceeds the allotted 50-minute lecture time to cover her slides, and she herself seems to lack an understanding of the course material. It feels like I'm being robbed of my money by SFU.

Poor professor, lacks understanding of the subject matter. The midterm focused solely on proofs, despite the fact that class instruction was on solving questions, not proving them. The class is unhelpful. Lectures are not recorded, and notes are not shared.

I've encountered unsupportive professors in previous semesters, but Jane's teaching is particularly problematic. She writes notes quickly and her handwriting is very messy, yet she forbids students from taking pictures of her notes. I regret not paying attention to the reviews.

Jane struggles to understand her own slides while explaining them. If you're going to teach a course, I'd expect you to practice and memorize your content better. It disrupts my learning and train of thought when I have to erase my notes to follow your corrections. This happens every lecture, by the way.

Jane can barely figure out her own slides when she's explaining it. If you're going to be teaching a course, I'd expect you to at least try to practice and memorize enough of your own content. It totally ruins my learning and train of thought. I hate that I have to erase my notes while following what you're fixing. This occurs every lecture btw

The course material seems fun, but she makes this class a disaster. It's impossible to take proper notes because of her handwriting and unnecessary strict policies (no photos). Professors are supposed to support students in learning, but she does the opposite, making the class impossible to learn properly.
i really wish she just posted her lecture notes! would make my life so much easier! exams are based entirely on them

I've had unsupportive professors in past semesters but Jane's teaching is unacceptable. She's a fast-paced note writer and has very messy handwriting, but prohibits everyone from taking pictures of her notes. Nearly every lecture she loses her train of thought and stammers for minutes on end (no exaggeration.) I wish I listened to the reviews.
Terrible professor
Worst prof I've ever seen. Doesn't post solutions to exams/assignments and doesn't post lecture slides. Scribbles on slides in class and expects us to decipher her notes.

Don't let the pretty(?) face distract you from the fact that she is truly the devil in disguise. I've never seen a more incompetent professor. It is evident that she doesn't have a clue about what she's teaching because of the numerous amount of blank stares at the board she does per lecture. Tests are bad. Disorganized. Doesn't post lecture notes

It does not matter how unfortunate your situation is. Please avoid this professer at all costs. She frequently makes mistakes during her lectures and costs us extra time. She does not provide filled out notes nor recorded lectures and if you did take notes her explanations are very bad. She is an EXTREMELY HARD/STRICT MARKER.

I have had bad experiences with professors at SFU but none of them were as bad as Jane, I have only finally made an account on here just to post this review. Cons: bad handwriting, does not allow taking pictures, unclear grading criteria. I am also sure that she would not be able to solve her own midterm in under 50 minutes unless she has the key

Jane doesn't seem to understand the content that she is teaching, and often pauses during her lectures to re-read what she has written. Class corrects her mistakes several times per lecture. CAs are on material not well taught during lecture. Be prepared to self study a lot, and not just by the textbook -- she barely follows that.

bad prof, doesnt know what shes teaching. midterm was not all testing on what she taught it was only proofs and in class we were taught how to solve questions not proof. Useless class. Lectures arent recorded, notes aren't shared.
its so frustrating being in a class where she is your prof.

Stammers and garbles. Many times has no idea what she's talking about. I am fine with her strict policies and all, but SFU should really use more competent lecturers, at least in key courses if there's too few ppl (lol). Being understaffed is one thing, grasping at straws is another

literally the most unprepared prof ive ever had. she doesnt care about the course and she is unable to teach it. she goes over the allotted 50 minutes of lecture time to go over her slides. she herself lacks an understanding of the course material. feels like I'm being robbed of my money from sfu.
Very difficult to read her mathematical formulas and handwriting.
Hey everyone, she is teaching again in summer 24. Avoid at all cost, I would rather delay graduation than do a course with her.

85% of the grading was test-based. Weekly quizzes often about obscure material not from the course textbook, which was the only source of knowledge since her lecture notes were useless and not uploaded. Computing assignments had asinine requirements, and points were often deducted with no good reasons. Just avoid Jane like the plague, please.
Recommend
Literally, one of the best professors at SFU. Very clear on what he talks about, well organized notes. I agree with prev. posts, he seems to love lecturing. Straightforward exams, fair marking.

Overall, he is a good prof. He is maybe not talented in teaching but really passionate and makes lots of effort into the course for students. Lectures are more theoretical, while tutorials are for problem solving. Assignments are very time consuming and expect you to use a Math software like Maple. Exams are fair where the class avg is about 65%.

The final exam is very difficult. many unexpected questions compared to a relatively easy midterm. you need to go to his class in order to earn attendance mark; no homework solution would be given by TA or himself; I don't recommend him at all
Very bad
HARD EXAM! DOESNT HELP! NO USE!

Has an attitude problem and never answers a simple e-mail. Had to go to the Mathematics director to get a response from him. He's a decent teacher but has poor organization skills. His marking breakdown didn't match  the one the website. He said at the start of semester that we could miss one homework and one quiz except he counted all of them.

Test were not bad, but he didn't scale as promised, and changed the marking scheme on us without telling anyone. Appears easy going in lecture, but is very difficult to deal with in person. Large chunk of mark was attendence, but he himself was away for several weeks. You can do well on tests and still get a bad grade.
Hard final exam compared to his midterm. Assignments require a lot of work. I needed a 0.25% to get a B+ and he REFUSED to bump me up! So he is very uncooperative and a bit annoying.
He didn't post solutions for the midterm or assignments. His final was not easy at all
Before I took this class I did not fully understand linear Algebra but the methods he showed us, were very clear and easy to understand.

Overall the course was fair, its more of a plain math course where you learn an algo. and you apply it. However, final seemed rather complex compared to midterm and homework. He is a good prof. giving examples for the algo. he teaches so its definitely more helpful than other profs that may not do that and expect u to learn it yourself.
Hard final exam :(

I thought Bob was awesome and he probably had a greater impact on me than any other professor in eight years of University (160 credits earned in five years at SFU).  I see negative comments.  The thing about numerical analysis is that it builds on calculus and algebra but it really is a different mindset from other math classes.
The course is easy and the exams are fair. He's helpful but his lecture is a little bit boring.

He's a great guy who simply cannot lecture well. Work hard to understand the concepts in the textbook--there's a lot of material in this class, and he's not afraid to test you on it (not just applying but understanding concepts). His exams are tough, but you get a formula sheet and scaling is generous.
He is a good teacher... Its just what he teaches is too complex and intense. can not blame him

This prof is not that bad as people think. He knows his stuff well, but doesn't know how to teach. The course itself is really hard, His exams are killer as well, but if you study hard and clear your concepts, you can do really well in this course.

His notes are no different than the text book.Never actually explain how to do assignment problems with computer(u've to learn to program urself). He sometimes will ask "how many is following me?" and only less than 5-8 students raise hand out of 100 students. His exams are difficult. no cheat sheets,open book and formula on the back of the test.

HE GIVES THE HARDEST EXAMS!!! DO NOT TAKE HIS CLASS IF YOU WANT TO RECEIVE A HIGH GRADE. ALL HIS LECTURE MATERIALS ARE FROM THE TEXTBOOK SO LEARNING FROM THE TEXT IS SAME THING AS GOING TO HIS CLASS. HORRIBLE CLASS!!!

HORRIBLE PROFESSOR! He mumbles all the time and does not explain the concepts properly. The exams are ridiculously difficult. He does not provide the solutions to sample exams and assignments. Hence, there is no way to know what the correct answer is. He mumbles all the time. This is my last sem at SFU and Bob Russel is the worst Prof at SFU!!
Just don't take this class with him.exams are extremely hard :(

Caring, inspirational professor who cares about his students and the material presented. Spend time on the material he teaches, be curious, ask questions - he will be there to help you. Steep learning curve, but overall, best course I have ever taken. Learned a lot.

He is kind and cares about our success. His lectures aren't very comprehensible from the first go, however upon rewatching youtube videos at 1.5x speed the material becomes clear. Have I taken macm316 live I would probably be totally lost. Trummer is very considerate and he made sure we do OK during this hard covid time.
Certainly not really good at teaching but the course is quite difficult so don't expect to score great without studying. He is really considerate and goes out of his way to help the students!

Lectures were hard to followed, but if you read through the text, thing became clear. Quizzes,Midterm,Final were not difficult as long as you done Homework. The course itself was supposed to be hard, but our class average was much higher than previous classes. Expect spending more than 10 hours a week to get an solid A+.

It is not an easy course but if you study hard, you can do a beautiful job. People can not expect a desired result if they don't even put effort on it. Go to office hour and tutorials, and post your questions on canvas discussions, Prof and TAs will come to help you very soon! They are quite supportive.

First lectures regarding computer arithmetic were relatively hard to follow, however course content became a lot clearer when it came to the linear algebra and ODE sections. A very kind professor with regards to grading and the grade cutoff ranges were more than generous. Quizzes were a hit-or-miss, as some were very easy, while some very hard.
Not a really good lecturer to be very honest, but he's a caring and understanding guy. 4/10 teaching 10/10 boomer humour

I came in with very low expectations due to the reviews on here, but ended up having a great learning experience. THE most caring and generous professors I've ever had. His lectures can be difficult to follow and he does often assume his students know more than they do. But he makes up for this with generous office hours and grading.

Although his lectures were a little fast, and some things could be explained better, he was a wonderful person overall. If you have any questions, he's always willing to go over things in more detail, which in my eyes makes up for the sometimes questionable lecture quality.
4/10 Teaching 9/10 Boomer humor

had this prof for macm 316. student attendance dropped from 60 to 10 because of his incomprehensible accent, inability to teach (just read the textbook word for word, and his 'notes' were straight from the textbook. i would rate him a 0 if it were possible
The midterm was okay. Bro railed me on the final; the class average was a 40%. No mercy.

This class was difficult, but Steve has many resources that make passing doable, such as online lecture recordings, and premade lecture notes that you can fill as you attend. The material will be a lot to grasp, but you have access to past exams to see what is expected of you in the midterm and final. And he is very easygoing and approachable. GLHF
Avoid at all cost

It was going great until the final. When out of nowhere he decided to make only our final, out of all the previous finals, extremely hard. The average was 42%. And midterm's average was 73%. Still salty about why he only chose to eff up our final out of all the previous terms he has taught this course. But overall, best prof for macm 316.

Course itself was hard but Steve made it manageable. Has lecture videos from Covid and all past midterms + finals with solutions available. Computing assignments are marked strictly, and Steve doesn't explain the course material well. Don't slack off ever + make the textbook your best friend is my best advice, if you listen you will do well, GL.
Fair marker and interesting lectures. The recorded lectures were quite good as well in case you missed class.

Online video lectures and other resources like tutorials, workshops, and office hours available to help you succeed. Midterm was reasonable, computing assignments in MATLAB are difficult and graded with scrutiny, final was difficult due to it being long and including proofs. Prof. Ruuth explains concepts well, but the class is still difficult.

Steve is an okay professor. Lectures are pretty boring, and Computing assignments are pretty difficult to do. The midterm was okay, but a few tricky questions. However, the final was extremely difficult as there were lots of proofs. The average grade was a B-.

Great prof, all the content you can find video recording and gives you past finals to practice, you can replace midterm grade with final's if you get better in final, scaled the final grade a lot in this sem, assignments are not that hard but the page limitation kinda annoying, sadly this sem's final is at least 3x harder than past finals.
bit hard marking computing assignments, BUT HES THE GOAT… THE GOAAAAATTT !!! EXAM JAIL , THOUGHT I GOT RAILED, BUT AN INSANE SCALE…. LAKERS IN 5
I never wore my seatbelt going to class because i hoped something would hit me on the way

Lectures are filled with proof I could not understand but too nice of a grader, Final grade was scaled up by two letter grades. Exams are not too bad but the way he teaches is really frustrating, good thing that this course has a lot of resources online

The prof can have messy handwriting at times. But he is pretty respectful and chill. Tests and assignments were also marked fairly, based on what was learned in the course, and the tutorials were super helpful in showing the steps used in working towards solutions for questions.

This is a tough class. His notes can be a bit unorganized and I found the grading to be very tough (not a lot of part marks). But I feel like he tried to explain things as best as he could and he was a nice enough guy. Not a bad choice for MACM 316. Hopefully he switches to using slides more.

Initially i did not look forward to this course but by the end of the semester I have a new-found respect for this prof (failed midterm but aced final, my fault entirely not hers). Lecture notes were crystal clear, office hours very helpful. Study off the notes and assignments and you should be fine.

Good enough professor, knows what he's talking about and takes feedback well. Topic is fairly dry but his delivery is good. Homework is reasonable and marking for coding assignments are pretty generous. Exams are on the long side, require memorization of formulas, and I was ultimately unable to finish both midterms.
Good prof
He cannot explain well, he just copy-pastes the questions and answers from his own notes and his marking is so unfair. and he scales down terribly. IGNORE AT ALL COST
He is very accessible and patient to answer students questions. Exams are easy if you do the work.
Best lectures!
I'd like to thank the professor for the amazing course!

I'm happy with the overall course experience provided by Prof. Liu. Lectures were interesting and inspiring. Contents were well organized and explained. Schedule and scheme were clearly laid out. Tutors are very supportive. I just thought I could've got a better grade. The prof is great!
He explained well and made the course so easy to learn. Do the work and its an easy A.
The prof gave amazing lectures combing math with a lot of applications! Grades are fair.

Prof. Liu is a very good professor. He explained the course well and made the lectures very accesible and easy to learn. He is also very caring. I found it is really helpful for an online course that he stayed 10-15 mins after each lecture just to answer students questions regarding the lecture. I learned a lot from him. He is a great!

Javier was one of the best professors I've had to date. As everyone else is saying, his lecture notes are great. He is very open to questions during and after class and has an overall amazing attitude. The course is hard but he gave lots of practice questions. Si estás leyendo esto Javier, gracias!

His lectures were great. I understood almost everything and rarely had to consult the textbook to learn the material. There is lots of unmarked HW. However, the computing assignments (CA) are marked surprisingly harsh, so take them seriously (proper report format with intro & conclusion). Exams reflected CAs and HW. Many sample exams are provided.

Javier's notes are so good I never even had to use the textbook except for the homework questions. He's a fantastic teacher and has a great way of making sure everyone understands before he moves on. You can just tell he genuinely cares about his students. His exams are completely fair, and the past exams he provided were a massive help.
You can tell he really knows his stuff. He made a dry subject/course tolerable and dare I say even enjoyable at times. An excellent prof.

Javier's lectures were extremely clear and I found them worth attending. Honestly the lecture slides were enough to study as they were much more helpful than the textbook explanations. HW is definitely worth doing as it appears on exams almost exactly. Low workload, I crammed at the end and still managed a decent grade. Javier is great!

Very solid, knowledgeable lectures, and delivers them quite concisely. Sometimes there's too much stuff to write down though, but not a problem for the most part. Exams are more than fair (but worth 75%). Computing assignments have extremely good feedback, but they may be marked slightly inconsistently.

Lectures were a bit hard to understand because of the amount of theory/proofs that was being presented. However, his examples would often clear things up. The homework was more practical than the lectures which helped with understanding the material. The exams were very fair and were based on HW and CA's.

Course is really well organized, exams are very fair, pretty good lectures. Only small con in my opinion is that the assignments feel really random on whether you'll get a good grade or not, as sometimes they're marked very harshly but other times really lightly.
He's chill, exam questions were very similar to past exams + hw which were extremely fair. Goated

Javier's notes and lectures are very clear, with lots of graphs/visualizations, so I had to refer to the textbook very few times. He provides lots of opportunities to ask questions. Reasonable workload. Exams are also very fair. Overall a solid professor for 316.
Final was very fair and similar to the textbook questions. He's super nice in class and explains concepts clearly. Highly recommended for 316.

I was scared to take MACM 316 because I heard its a beast in itself. However Javier is a great lecturer, clear notes, answers lots of questions in class and midterm was fair based on what we learned. Computing assignments took more time and were graded a little harsh but overall Javier is awesome. Definitely reccommend him as a prof.

It was Javier's first time teaching this course and I thought he did a great job. A couple cons: computing assignments were graded very harshly with weird/vague criteria, and the course itself is very heavy on notation/proofs. But Javier explained stuff pretty well and was really approachable for questions.
Avoid this professor at all costs
